items.title: Cats, Eagles meet at Norwood Oval ahead of Gather Round finale

children.content: Geelong and West Coast meet at suburban Norwood Oval to kick off the last day of Gather Round, ahead of the Giants and Tigers in the Barossa and a Power-Saints finale at Adelaide Oval. Follow live.

articles.title: Fans living it up in Adelaide for Gather Round but future of event uncertain

children.content: Occupancy rates and food and drink sales are up on last year's Gather Round, says the Australian Hotels Association, despite concerns fuel prices would deter interstate visitors.

image.altText: People in different club attires walk on a footbridge with Gather Round signs. A lit up Adelaide Oval in the distance at night

gallery.altText: Shadows cross a traditional Australian Rules football ground at Norwood in Adelaide, with an old grandstand behind the goals.

thumbnail.altText: Shadows cross a traditional Australian Rules football ground at Norwood in Adelaide, with an old grandstand behind the goals.

items.title: Independent supermarkets struggle to keep prices down as Iran war hurts

children.content: Suppliers are charging more to deliver groceries to supermarkets as fuel prices increase, putting pressure on smaller retailers.

image.altText: Fresh vegetables on three black shelves with price stickers in front of the items.

items.title: 'Epic in scale': APY Lands exhibition opens at NGA after three-year delay

children.content: An exhibition of First Nations art from the APY Lands in South Australia brings together 30 large-scale works by nearly 50 artists.

image.altText: Two woman sitting on while painting.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: From No Pokies to no MPs: Last SA Best member proud of party's legacy after losing seat

children.content: SA Best, the party formed by stunt-loving politician Nick Xenophon, has closed its chapter in South Australian political history with its final member Connie Bonaros losing her seat in last month's state election.

gallery.altText: A woman with curly blonde hair standing with a cut-out of a man wearing an orange vest

thumbnail.altText: A woman with curly blonde hair standing with a cut-out of a man wearing an orange vest

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

image.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: Bricks and brutalism being added to heritage list, as owners raise concerns

children.content: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

image.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof

items.title: The fourth-year med student from Broken Hill who won the Stawell Gift

children.content: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

gallery.altText: Olufemi Komolafe holds the trophy after winning the 2026 Men's Stawell Gift

thumbnail.altText: Olufemi Komolafe holds the trophy after winning the 2026 Men's Stawell Gift

items.title: SA GP with an 'unhealthy obsession' for laser therapy disqualified

children.content: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

image.altText: Close-up photo of a silver and white stethoscope. Dec 2022.

items.title: Commuters trapped for hours in freeway gridlock blast lack of signage

children.content: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

items.title: 'Preferred profit … over the environment': Farm owner fined for clearing 112 hectares of bushland

children.content: The owner of a farm in South Australia's south-east is convicted and fined for clearing 112 hectares of bushland, but the penalty is much less than the $2.2 million he would have had to pay for a permit for the work.

image.altText: A small bird with grey and gold plumage with a black chin

items.title: Four charged for alleged offences 'connected' to murder of Adelaide father Chris Robertson

children.content: Four people have been arrested by police for alleged offences "connected" to the alleged murder of Adelaide father-of-two Chris Robertson last year.

image.altText: A photo of Chris Robertson whose death is being treated as murder.

items.title: Trucks about to get longer, heavier in bid to reduce fuel costs

children.content: The South Australian government says recent changes to heavy vehicle freight limits will lower fuel costs and reduce the number of freight trucks needed on the road.

image.altText: an image of a car's driver side mirror with a truck reflected, driving through a town

items.title: Festival chief warned author's removal would 'decimate' Writers' Week

children.content: An extraordinary series of emails sheds light on the reservations that preceded, and internal fallout that followed, the decision to scrap Randa Abdel-Fattah's Writers' Week appearance.

image.altText: Emails obtained under FOI relating to Adelaide Writers' Week.

items.title: The biggest group of kids at risk of falling through NDIS cracks

children.content: With just months to go until Thriving Kids starts, parents and experts are warning a particular cohort of children is at risk of falling through the cracks.

gallery.altText: Two young boys sitting infront of their mum on a bench at a playground

thumbnail.altText: Two young boys sitting infront of their mum on a bench at a playground

items.title: A budget 'leak' so good, the journalist's editors didn't believe it

children.content: It was "the best Canberra scoop in years", but the journalist's own editors did not believe it, and almost buried the story.

items.title: Head swelled to 'basketball size': How it feels to eject from a fighter jet

children.content: A retired F-15 pilot who survived one of the fastest known fighter jet ejections in history, describes what two US airmen would have faced when they did the same in Iran a week ago.

image.altText: A pilot being catapulted into the air from an aircraft showing jets from the launch explosion underneath.

items.title: I thought I understood China. Then I ordered a craft beer

children.content: On a midsummer night last year, I took a sip of lager at a craft beer pub in my hometown in China's south. But this was not just a beer. It was a cultural symbol that shows just how much China's Gen Z is changing.

gallery.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

thumbnail.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

items.title: In the Gulf, states are 'paying the price' for a war they didn't start

children.content: When the Iran war ends, Gulf nations will not only have to repair the economic and physical damage caused by Iran's retaliatory campaign, but will also have to learn to live with a regime that analysts say is "less predictable and more violent than it was two months ago".

image.altText: Missiles produced by Iran's armed forces.

items.title: Kennedy breaks 10 seconds again to claim first national title in men's 100m

children.content: Lachlan Kennedy storms to victory in the men's 100m final at the Australian Athletics Championships in Sydney, equalling his personal best of 9.96 seconds.

image.altText: Sprinter Lachlan Kennedy has both fists pumped and a satisfied look on his face after winning the 100m final

items.title: 'It's taken me all over the world': Life as a second-generation human cannonball

children.content: David Smith grew up watching his father being shot out of a cannon, so when the time came, he said it was inevitable he would follow in his dad's flight path.

image.altText: David "The Bullet" Smith being shot out of a cannon at the 2026 Sydney Royal Easter Show.

gallery.altText: A man is shot out of a smoking cannon in a spray of golden sparks.

thumbnail.altText: A man is shot out of a smoking cannon in a spray of golden sparks.

items.title: The nannasphere is an antidote to the world's grief and horrors

children.content: Surely one of the best ways to counter the hate, grief and horrors of the world is to foster communities driven by decency, calm and care. Enter: the nannasphere.

image.altText: A group of women wearing bright yellow, hold signs like 'defend the right to protest' outside the NSW Supreme Court.

avatar.alt: A close up photo of Julia Baird, who hair bronde hair and is wearing a navy blazer and blue shirt

items.title: Still trying to prove you're good at trivia? You're not fooling me

children.content: I applaud your courage for having a go at this quiz. That's about it, though.

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

items.title: 'John Cena meets Jesus' in bold new exhibition by disabled artists

children.content: Paper dolls, wrestlers and cloudscapes appear in an exhibition by a Western Sydney artist collective at Bathurst Regional Art Gallery.

gallery.altText: Dress the Depressed by Leanne Larwood featuring a life-size picture of a woman surrounded by different clothing items.

thumbnail.altText: Dress the Depressed by Leanne Larwood featuring a life-size picture of a woman surrounded by different clothing items.
